1.premise: Rolling down a window to give a homeless man money implies charity, but reveals the homeless man offering water back.
"Rear-ended" can mean struck from behind in a car accident or penetrated anally.
Financial desperation is so extreme that winning $20 million wouldn't be worth the risk of a five-dollar ticket.

lines.text: You know what degrading it is to have a homeless man put his
lines.label: setup

lines.text: sign down when he sees your car pull up?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Yeah, having me on my way here.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I pulled off on Fifth Street, and this homeless guy just--
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They'd be homeless, but they're not dumb.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I rolled my window down, tried giving him money.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: He tried handing me a water bottle.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: That's how bad it is for me, seriously.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: I drive a really old, beat up 2002 Honda Sierra V.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: It's been rear-ended probably as many times as Tony.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I'm also like dumb broke though. Like I won't even buy a Powerball ticket till it's at least at 1 billion
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I'm gonna risk five bucks for 20 million dollars, you know, like it's not really gonna do for me
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Happy Black History Month guys for those who celebrate not this guy apparently it's okay
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: So got like a week and a half left
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: All right, I'll send it there. Thank you
lines.label: fluff
